text:
1/ 5 days. 40 lines. One silent state bug in an agent loop. Here's what debugging it taught me.

2/ Symptom: tasks disappearing from the queue. No error, no log, just gone. Model was innocent — the state machine ate them.

3/ Root cause: handlers returned partial state deltas. Anything not in the delta got dropped on merge. Fix: append-only patches, no object replacements.

4/ Adjacent bug: budget check happened before charge, so every run overshot by exactly one call. Off-by-one is cheap to write, expensive to catch.

5/ Real lesson: the interesting work in agent systems is never the model. It's the plumbing. Budget every loop. Log every delta. Tag every retry with a reason code.
